utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . next patient is michelle king . date of birth : 05/07/1982 . please use toc template . she was admitted to district regional hospital on 06/29/2021 , and was discharged on 07/02/2021 . the patient is a 39-year-old female with history of quadriplegia due to , uh , prior spinal cord injury . she was experiencing increasing concerns for spasms and lightheadedness , significant constipation , and reported diaphoresis . she was evaluated in the emergency department where she was , uh , febrile and had significantly elevated white blood cell count of 23,000 . there were concerns for ongoing urinary tract infection , but she was treated with iv antibiotic therapy . she had labile blood pressure , headache , and vomiting . in the end , she slowly improved . she does have a supra-pubic catheter . cultures were obtained and reviewed . she was eventually discharged after clinical improvement of her symptoms . she presents today to follow up regarding these concerns . hi , michelle . how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not too bad . and yourself ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm good , thanks . so , it looks like you had an infection , right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . but you know , i really do n't think it was a uti .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i told them when i went to the er ... is that your phone ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it is , yeah . it listens to you and me and it does all of my documentation . it's pretty good stuff . um , tell me what's going on .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it is , in my humble opinion , that i was just constipated and that was what was causing my blood pressure to be so high . and i hardly pooped when i was there . then when i got home i had a massive poop and i felt much better . i told them that when i was admitted , and they did x-rays and everything , but they said , " no , " as they did n't think that was n't ... that was it . but you know , i felt so much better when i got home and finally pooped .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , and the white cell count was the only thing that i think they were kind of concerned about because it was a little high .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i wondered if automatic dysreflexia can affect the white count ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: typically , it does n't .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but , i mean , when i looked at the urine cultures that they checked , the cultures are not very impressive .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that's what they said .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and when i go back and look , sometimes your white count is just elevated a little bit , and last year it was normal , so ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but in saying that , you know , inflammation can do that . so , yeah , you could be backed up and inflamed .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , they did n't do a cat scan , they just did an x-ray . but you feel like you're back to your normal self now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i mean , i went in because i knew my blood pressure was out of control .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it was giving me a headache and making me throw up . and i know , at that point , i'm at the loss of what i can do at home .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , it was good that you went in .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i went in and they made me feel better , and then i went home , as you know .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , maybe that's all it was .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so you're doing good otherwise ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . let me take a quick listen to you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . go ahead and take a few deep breaths . and breathe normally . now take several deep breaths . and two more . okay , good . you can relax . any pain or discomfort anywhere ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . so here are my thoughts .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you may recall it was extremely hot during that period leading up to you going to the hospital . i do n't know if you were inside for the most part ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it was pretty hot , was n't it ? and i go out a bit , but i do tend to stay in more when it's hot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , if you do get dehydrated even just a little bit it can lead to further , um , or worsening constipation issues .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i've had a lot of patients over the last couple of weeks coming in with just weird stuff , and i blame that a little bit on how hot and humid it was the last two weeks of june . so what i would do is wait a couple of weeks and then , if you could , come back in and we could do some , uh , complete blood work , uh , including your white blood cells and a full panel of cholesterol , since it's been a while .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and it should normalize , but it's too soon to do it now . um , but maybe if i put some labs in you could come in in two to three weeks ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure , i can do that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . so that appointment will let us kill a bird ... two birds with one stone since you're due to get blood work anyways for cholesterol .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then , i just wanted to make sure that the white count is okay , because if it remains elevated then that means there's something else going on with your bowels .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i do n't think that's what we'll find , but we'll see .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean , i knew going in that i had n't had a regular bowel movement in a while , even though i drink the miralax every day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: when i do n't go , i double up on that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have i ever given you lactulose ? it would be a medicine you use in , um , conjunction with miralax . sort of a rescue type thing .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: is that the stuff that comes in a bottle ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: then , yes . i have it . it takes about two days to work and then it's a brutal two days .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: got it . yeah , it can be a little rough , but it works .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so we have our plan . any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , well , i would like to talk to you about covid , if we can .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure . so where are you at when it comes to the vaccine ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i am unvaccinated .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so what are your concerns about the vaccine ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , we do n't know the side effects .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: correct , we do n't .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's too soon to tell the side effects , and i'm hearing more and more reports of people getting what's now termed as " vaccine injury " .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: which i had never heard of before . i do n't know if it is a new term or not , but my take on the whole thing is that i'm young and healthy enough to beat it , even if my respiratory is limited . i also take a lot of vitamin c.

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i also take multivitamins and i'm closely monitored . so i guess i do n't feel as though i really the vaccine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , my thoughts are that you actually should be vaccinated . i think you are high risk . if you get covid it's not going to be good for you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i think it's safe . i mean , i understand and hear you . all of these stories out there about this , uh , regarding the vaccine . but i've seen all the bad things that could happen from covid . people have died in my practice , and i do n't disagree that if you got covid you'd probably be okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but i would consider you as someone that would make me a little bit concerned . i have had healthy people die from covid .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i've had some people get really , really sick from it . so personally , i believe that this vaccine is completely safe . i really think that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all of them ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i do , i do . i think there are side effects from all vaccines , so you have to go back and think about it . do you get flu shot every year ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i do n't .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right , well , it's hard for me to convince you because you're making a consistent viewpoint .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i am open-minded to this .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm . to me , it's , you know , a lot of the stuff out there is fear and most of us health providers have gotten the vaccine . we've seen what the virus can do . i also have gotten all of my kids vaccinated .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i'm not concerned about the side effects of this . i do n't think it does anything . um , and we have vaccinated 50 % of the country .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: the er is full of these vaccines .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i understand .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so there are definitely potential side effects to the vaccine . i mean , there's guillain-barre , and all these things that could happen from the vaccine , but they're just very rare . my thing for you is i'm not convinced that there's alternative stuff or even the treatment we have , like the monoclonal antibodies , that will give help , but it's just my opinion .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , that's what i'm here for .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . right now the numbers are low . even with the delta variant , when it starts mutating , it's actually good because it becomes more contagious , but less deadly .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so that's what's gon na happen with covid . that's what's mo- most of the science supports and this is how most pandemics end , is that the virus starts losing its potency .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but think about it , and if you have any more questions you can certainly let me know , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: will do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . anything else we can discuss today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope . i think i'm all set . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . so we'll see you in two to three weeks . have a good rest of your day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thanks , you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: next line heading will be " acute to chronic constipation " . she believes her hospitalization was more likely due to her dysreflexia , from her quadriplegia that typically occurs . she did have a lot of vital signs changes , including blood pressure fluctuations . the patient feels like she has a good bowel regimen , including taking the miralax regularly as a primary treatment for constipation issues as well as lactulose for severe constipation . she feels like this was less likely a true urinary tract infection and was just related to her constipation . however , we did have a good discussion regarding her leukocytosis .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i would recommend just in the next three or four weeks to come back in for some updated fasting labs , and we'll repeat the cbc just to make sure her white blood cells tend downward . uh , next line and heading will be " quadriplegia " . um , next line and heading will be " covid vaccine status " . again , she's hesitant . we had a good discussion , and i recommended that the only true preventative mechanism for covid is the vaccine . i do not believe that supplements are necessarily going to help stave this off . i would be concerned if she does n't get the vaccine and i would consider her at high risk based on her quadriplegia status . she does not want to get covid . however , she needs to make these decisions . she is somewhat homeopathic .

detail_list.summary: Michelle King is a 39-year-old female with a history of quadriplegia and spinal cord injury presenting for a transition of care visit following a recent hospitalization.
The patient was hospitalized from 06/29/2021 to 07/02/2021 for symptoms including spasms, lightheadedness, constipation, diaphoresis, labile blood pressure, headache, and vomiting.
During hospitalization, the patient was febrile, had a white blood cell count of 23,000, and was treated with IV antibiotics for a suspected urinary tract infection.
Infection follow-up
The patient does not believe she had a urinary tract infection.
The patient believes her symptoms were caused by constipation and autonomic dysreflexia rather than an infection.
The doctor notes the patient's elevated white blood cell count was a primary concern during the hospitalization.
The patient asks if autonomic dysreflexia can cause an elevated white blood cell count.
The doctor states that autonomic dysreflexia typically does not cause an elevated white blood cell count.
The doctor notes that the urine cultures from the hospitalization were not impressive.
The doctor suggests that inflammation from constipation could contribute to an elevated white blood cell count.
The doctor performs a physical examination of the patient's lungs and finds no pain or discomfort.
The doctor recommends the patient return in two to three weeks for fasting labs, including a CBC and cholesterol panel.
The patient reports taking MiraLAX daily for constipation.
The doctor asks if the patient has been prescribed Lactulose.
The patient confirms she uses Lactulose as needed, noting it takes two days to produce a bowel movement.
COVID-19 vaccination
The patient is unvaccinated against COVID-19.
The patient expresses concern regarding unknown side effects of the COVID-19 vaccine.
The patient reports taking vitamin C and a multivitamin, and believes she is healthy enough to beat COVID-19 despite limited respiratory status.
The doctor advises the patient to get vaccinated, noting she is at high risk due to her quadriplegia.
The doctor asks if the patient receives an annual flu vaccine.
The patient denies receiving the flu vaccine.

case_description: follow up visit; quadriplegia; covid-19 vaccine
